activities.text_title: Heroes Every Child Should Know: Perseus

questions.question: What does Perseus's response in paragraph 5 reveal about his character?

responses.content: Perseus agrees to attempt and slay Medusa.

questions.question: Look back at Excerpt 2. How does Perseus's response to the Grey Sisters help advance his quest?

responses.content: Perseus character is very brave and i feel this because no other normal person would agree to slay a monster in hell

questions.question: What does Perseus's victory over Medusa mean for his quest?

responses.content: Because if Perseus doesn’t have the hat of darkness he wont be able to turn invisible

questions.question:
How do Perseus's responses to individuals and events drive the action of the plot forward? 


Respond to this question in the form of a paragraph that includes a claim, one piece of evidence, and an explanation.

responses.content: Perseus responses to individual and events drive the action of the plot forward? First it all started with athene and athene gave Perseus his first direction on how to slay Medusa, and he agreed. So  athene gave Perseus a mirror like sheild and a long sword to cut medusas head off. Then Perseus saw Hermes and Hermes gave persues special sandals to fly. Then Perseus saw the 3 gray sisters and took they eye saying he wont give it back unless they tell him where to find gorgon and from there he goes to Medusa and is successful in killing Medusa so he goes back and gives medusas head to athene as he was told to.   It says all this in the story too this is how persues moves the storyline and plot forward by meeting new people and following directions

It says all this in the story too this is how persues moves the storyline and plot forward by meeting new people and following directions and this moves the plot forward by persues following step by step and going to new places and by the end he is successful
